more.title: EFF wants parly to probe NSFAS administrator’s unapproved spending spree
more.description: EFF MP Sihle Lonzi has written to Parliament’s Portfolio Committee on Higher Education spokesperson Tebogo Letsie to allow the committee to get involved in probing whether NSFAS administrator Prof. Hlengani Mathebula was spending public funds without approval by finance minister Enoch Godongwana as stipulated in law. The EFF letter comes after Sunday World broke the […]

more.title: Mixed reactions as seven ex-MECs return to power in Mpumalanga
more.description: The return of some of Mpumalanga’s most recognisable political figures to positions of influence has reopened an old debate about experience versus recycling, following the provincial government’s unveiling of new boards for two of its flagship economic agencies. The appointment of several former Mpumalanga MECs and ANC political heavyweights to the boards of the Mpumalanga […]

more.title: National Treasury freezes July grant payments to more than 60 municipalities
more.description: The national purse's decision aims to protect public funds and enforce accountability after the municipalities failed to meet legal and financial management requirements.

more.title: South Africa's data breaches could be costing the economy R142 billion a year
more.description: South Africa's more than 3,200 reported data breaches have cost the economy an estimated R142 billion, with experts warning that simple employee training could prevent many incidents.

more.title: South Africa Migrant Crisis: Stranded Foreign Nationals Seek Repatriation Amid Rising Tensions
more.description: CAPE TOWN, Western Cape – The escalating South Africa migrant crisis has left hundreds of illegal immigrants and foreign nationals stranded in makeshift camps across Cape Town and Durban. As government authorities halt repatriation transport, vulnerable individuals are facing severe humanitarian challenges, compounded by rising local intimidation that has forced many out of their homes [...]

more.title: South Africa Immigration Crisis: Save the Children Warns of Severe Risks to Migrant Children During Repatriation
more.description: JOHANNESBURG, SOUTH AFRICA — As the South Africa immigration crisis intensifies and the repatriation process continues, the protection and safety of migrant children have emerged as a critical humanitarian concern. Gugu Xaba, CEO of Save the Children South Africa, has highlighted the severe vulnerabilities faced by migrant families, particularly those with children born in the [...]

more.description: Proteas all-rounder Marizanne Kapp has been named in the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup 2026 Team of the Tournament.

more.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa says South Africa is emerging as a leading digital investment destination, with major commitments from global technology companies expected to accelerate economic growth, job creation and artificial intelligence (AI) innovation. more.title: South Africa to Host Landmark 2026 SADC Industrialisation Week in Durban
more.description: DURBAN, KwaZulu-Natal — South Africa is preparing to welcome regional and international delegates to the Durban International Convention Centre later this month for the 2026 Annual SADC Industrialisation Week (SIW), a flagship event positioned as the largest public-private platform driving industrial development across the Southern African Development Community. Scheduled for July 27–31, 2026, the week-long [...]

more.title: Brazen passenger arrested with 50kg of khat at Cape Town airport
more.description: A 46-year-old man has been arrested after police discovered more than 50kg of khat concealed in his luggage at Cape Town International Airport.

more.title: South Africans crowned the world’s most wildlife obsessed travellers
more.description: For many South Africans, planning a holiday isn’t just about beaches, luxury hotels or famous landmarks—it’s about the chance to see incredible wildlife up close, Cape {town} Etc reports. more.title: Regis Resources meets top end of full-year gold guidance
more.description: ASX-listed Regis Resources has delivered a 12% quarter-on-quarter increase in gold production for the three months ended June 30. The quarter's output of 101 500 oz brings the group's total 2026 financial year production to 379 000 oz - just shy of the company's top-end of guidance of 380 000 oz.
